snp2.13:1.1: “I ask the sage of abundant discernment,
snp2.13:1.2: crossed over to the far shore, totally unbound, steadfast in mind:
snp2.13:1.4:
how does one wander rightly in the world?”
The Buddha:
snp2.13:2.4: whose fault of omens is completely abandoned:
snp2.13:2.5: He would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:3.1: A monk should subdue passion for sensualities human
snp2.13:3.3: Having gone past becoming, and met with the Dhamma,
snp2.13:3.4: he would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:4.2: a monk should abandon anger & meanness.
snp2.13:4.4: he would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:5.4: he would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:6.1: He finds no essence in acquisitions,
snp2.13:6.3: independent is he, by others unled:
snp2.13:6.4:
He would wander rightly in the world.
Having rightly found the Dhamma,
snp2.13:7.1: he is unobstructed in speech, mind, & act.
snp2.13:7.4: he would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:8.1:
A monk who’d not gloat,
“He venerates me,”
snp2.13:8.3: or be elated on receiving food from another:
snp2.13:8.4: He would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:9.3: he, having crossed over doubt, de-arrowed,
snp2.13:9.4: he would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:10.1: Having found what’s appropriate for himself,
snp2.13:10.2: the monk wouldn’t harm anyone in the world,
snp2.13:10.3: Having found the Dhamma as it actually is,
snp2.13:10.4: he would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:11.1: In whom there are no obsessions,
snp2.13:11.4: He would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:12.2: beyond reach of every road to passion,
snp2.13:12.3: tamed, totally unbound, steadfast in mind:
snp2.13:12.4: He would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:13.2: not following factions among those who are factious, enlightened;
snp2.13:13.4: He would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:14.1: Victorious, pure, his roof opened up,
snp2.13:14.2: a master of dhammas, gone beyond & unperturbed,
snp2.13:14.3: skilled in the knowledge of fabrication-cessation:
snp2.13:14.4: He would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:15.1: Gone beyond speculations about futures & pasts,
snp2.13:15.2: and—having passed by— purified in his discernment,
snp2.13:15.4: He would wander rightly in the world.
snp2.13:16.1: Knowing the state, meeting the Dhamma,
snp2.13:16.2: seeing the opened-up when his effluents are abandoned
snp2.13:16.3: from the ending of all acquisitions:
snp2.13:16.4: He would wander rightly in the world.”
snp2.13:17.1: “Yes, Blessed One, that’s just how it is.
snp2.13:17.2: Any monk dwelling thus, tamed, gone totally beyond
snp2.13:17.4: He would wander rightly in the world.”
